articles.full_text:
Temperatures near 45°C in Rajasthan:Storm, rain, and heatwave alerts issued for multiple districts
- Copy link
Amid the ongoing heatwave and scorching temperatures in Rajasthan, the cycle of dust storms and rain continues across the state. On Thursday, people faced intense heat and hot winds from morning till afternoon, but several districts, including Jaipur, later received rainfall.
According to the Meteorological Department, similar weather conditions are likely on Friday as well. However, after May 16, Rajasthan may witness continuous intense heat and heatwave conditions for nearly a week.
The department has issued a yellow alert for dust storms and rain in 10 districts on Friday. An orange alert for heatwave has also been issued in three districts, while one district remains under yellow alert for heatwave conditions.
Weather picture in Rajasthan
Day temperature still around 45°C
Relief in Jaipur with heavy rain
In the capital, a strong dust storm with speeds of about 30 to 40 km/h occurred in the evening. Due to heavy rain in most parts of the city, the temperature dropped by about 5 to 6 degrees, reaching 36 degrees Celsius.
Weather conditions in major cities of Rajasthan
Jaipur: The daytime temperature in Jaipur dropped by more than 1 degree, with the maximum recorded at 42°C. The weather remained mostly clear with occasional clouds during the day, while heavy rain lashed the city late in the evening. The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for storm and rain in Jaipur on May 15.
Jodhpur: Jodhpur recorded a daytime temperature of around 44°C on Thursday. Amid the scorching heat, water was sprayed around the Defence Minister’s rally venue, where even children stopped near the cooling machines for relief.
Kota: People are struggling with intense sunlight and hot winds from around 11 AM. The maximum temperature reached 43.4°C, and many residents were seen searching for shade to escape the heat.
Ajmer: Strong sunlight and hot winds troubled residents in Ajmer from Thursday morning. Due to the severe heatwave, streets appeared deserted in the afternoon. The city recorded a maximum temperature of 42°C and a minimum of 25.4°C, while low humidity made conditions feel even hotter.
Udaipur: Udaipur recorded a maximum temperature of 42.1°C and a minimum of 25.5°C on May 14. A day earlier, the maximum temperature was 42.4°C and the minimum was 26°C. Hot winds continued to sweep through the city during the day.
Alwar: Clouds covered Alwar from Thursday morning, but residents got little relief as the maximum temperature still touched 40°C. People were seen adopting different methods to cope with the heat.
Sikar: Cloud cover and humidity persisted in Sikar since Thursday morning. After a hot day, the weather changed by evening, with strong winds and drizzle reported in several areas. The minimum temperature was recorded at 22.5°C.
